title: ICMR-NIN Releases Policy Priorities for Healthier Diets in Children and Adolescents
summary: The ICMR-National Institute of Nutrition (NIN)-led 'Let's Fix Our Food' (LFOF) Consortium released a policy document titled 'Priority Policy Actions to Promote Healthier Diets and Food Environments for Children and Adolescents in India'. Launched by NITI Aayog, the document provides evidence-based recommendations to improve dietary behaviors and food environments. The LFOF is a multi-institutional platform involving partners such as WHO, UNICEF India, FSSAI, PHFI, and Resolve to Save Lives (RTSL).

title: ICMR Transfers Indigenous Biomedical Technologies for Cancer and Enteric Infections
summary: Under the 'Medical Innovations Patent Mitra' initiative, ICMR licensed three indigenous technologies to Indian manufacturers. Key transfers include: 1) SHetA2, a first-in-class targeted therapeutic candidate for Cervical Intraepithelial Neoplasia (CIN) and cervical cancer, licensed to Emcure Pharmaceuticals Ltd. 2) Two next-generation vaccine technologies against enteric bacterial infections, developed at ICMR-NIRBI Kolkata and licensed to Biological E Ltd. These transfers aim to translate government-funded research into affordable healthcare solutions.

title: CDSCO Approves India's First Dengue Vaccine: Qdenga
summary: CDSCO has granted marketing authorization for Qdenga®, the first recombinant live attenuated tetravalent dengue vaccine approved in India. Manufactured by Takeda GmbH, it is indicated for individuals aged 4–60 years. The vaccine is produced in Vero cells using a dengue virus type-2 backbone and contains GMOs. Administration is via subcutaneous injection; the recommended schedule is two 0.5 ml doses administered 0 and 3 months apart.

title: Update on National Immunization Coverage and Pandemic Preparedness
summary: India has maintained National Full Immunization Coverage (FIC) above 90% for four years: 94.7% (22-23), 93.7% (23-24), 98.3% (24-25), and 97.9% (25-26). A nationwide HPV vaccination campaign launched Feb 28, 2026, targeting ~1.2 crore 14-year-old girls across 36 States/UTs. Other updates include the launch of an Integrated Training Module for Primary Healthcare Teams at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs (April 2026) and ICMR's network of 160+ Virus Research and Diagnostic Laboratories for pathogen diagnosis.

title: Progress Report on Nationwide HPV Vaccination and Cervical Cancer Prevention
summary: As of July 15, 2026, 5,210,302 girls have received the HPV vaccine under the campaign launched Feb 28, 2026, targeting 1.2 crore 14-year-old girls. Vaccines are provided free at government facilities, including Ayushman Arogya Mandirs, PHCs, CHCs, and GMCs. Vaccination data is tracked via the U-WIN digital portal. The government's comprehensive cervical cancer strategy integrates HPV vaccination, screening, early diagnosis at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs, and treatment through Day Care Cancer Centres.

title: NCAHP Releases Competency-Based Curriculum for DMLT
summary: The National Commission for Allied and Healthcare Professions (NCAHP), MoHFW, has released a Competency-Based Curriculum for the Diploma in Medical Laboratory Technology (DMLT). Shifting from knowledge-based to a framework emphasizing Knowledge, Skills, Values, and Attitudes (KSVA), the curriculum focuses on pre-analytical, analytical, and post-analytical phases of laboratory testing. Implementation begins in Academic Year 2026–27, with mandatory adoption by 2027–28. This initiative aligns with the Union Budget 2026–27 goal to create one lakh additional Allied and Healthcare Professionals over five years. The two-year program is for Class XII Science (PCB) graduates.

title: ICMR i-DRONE Initiative Reduces TB Diagnosis Time and Costs in Telangana
summary: An ICMR study under the i-DRONE initiative in Yadadri-Bhuvanagiri, Telangana, demonstrated that drone-assisted transport of sputum samples from PHCs/SCs to diagnostic laboratories significantly improves TB service access. In a study of 840 participants, the median turnaround time for diagnosis dropped from 15 days to 5 days. Furthermore, the mean out-of-pocket expenditure (OOPE) for patients decreased drastically from approximately ₹9,451 to ₹91, primarily due to reduced travel costs and wage loss. The project was conducted in collaboration with AIIMS Bibinagar and the National TB Elimination Programme (NTEP).

title: MoHFW Launches Monthly Webinar Series on NHM Best Practices
summary: Launched on World Population Day (July 11, 2026), the Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare introduced a Monthly Webinar Series under the National Health Mission (NHM). The initiative serves as a knowledge-sharing platform for States and UTs to document, exchange, and replicate scalable, evidence-based healthcare innovations to strengthen public health systems. The inaugural session, chaired by Smt. Aradhana Patnaik (Additional Secretary and Mission Director, NHM), focused on Anemia, Child, and Adolescent Health, with over 500 officials participating.

title: Amendment to Drugs Rules 1945: Regulation of High Alcohol-Containing Formulations
summary: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare has amended the Drugs Rules, 1945, to prevent misuse of high-alcohol medicinal products. Formulations containing >12% v/v ethyl alcohol in quantities >30 mL are removed from Schedule K (licensing exemption) and moved to Schedule H1. Consequently, these products now require valid licenses under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940, and must be dispensed only against a prescription from a Registered Medical Practitioner (RMP) with strict record-keeping requirements.

title: National ToT for Primary Healthcare Cadres at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs
summary: The Union Ministry of Health and Family Welfare conducted a National Training of Trainers (NToT) for five primary healthcare cadres operating at Ayushman Arogya Mandirs (AAMs). The initiative introduces an integrated training strategy and cadre-specific modules focusing on technical knowledge, behavioral competencies, empathy, and communication. The program will be implemented via a decentralized cascade model through over 100 Regional Training Institutes starting August 2026 to strengthen the primary healthcare workforce and improve service delivery through inter-agency convergence.
